member.name: T:Unknown
member.summary: Sets the Contract instance to call.

member.name: T:Unknown_2
member.summary: Set the 20-byte EVM address of the Contract to call.

member.name: T:Unknown_3
member.summary: Sets the Sender of the transaction simulation.

member.name: T:Unknown_4
member.summary: Set the 20-byte EVM address of the Sender.

member.name: T:Unknown_5
member.summary:
Sets the amount of Value (in tinybars or wei) to be sent to the Contract in the transaction.

Use this to specify an amount for a payable function call.

the amount of Value to send, in tinybars or wei {@code this}

member.name: T:Unknown_6
member.summary:
Sets the Gas limit for the Contract call.

This specifies the maximum amount of Gas that the transaction can consume.

the maximum Gas allowed for the transaction {@code this}

member.name: T:Unknown_7
member.summary:
Sets the Gas price to be used for the Contract call.

This specifies the price of each unit of Gas used in the transaction.

the Gas price, in tinybars or wei, for each unit of Gas {@code this}

member.name: M:FillEvmAddresses
member.summary:
Sets the block number for the simulation of the Contract call.

The block number determines the context of the Contract call simulation within the blockchain.

the block number at which to simulate the Contract call {@code this}

member.name: M:Estimate(Client)
member.summary: Returns Gas estimation for the EVM execution

member.name: T:MirrorNodeContractQuery
member.summary: MirrorNodeContractQuery returns a result from EVM execution such as cost-free execution of read-only smart Contract queries, Gas estimation, and transient simulation of read-write operations.

member.name: M:MirrorNodeContractQuery.SetFunction(System.String)
member.summary:
Sets the function name to call.

The function will be called with no parameters. Use {@link #setFunction(String, ContractFunctionParameters)} to call a function with parameters.

The String to be set as the function name {@code this}

member.name: M:MirrorNodeContractQuery.SetFunction(System.String,ContractFunctionParameters @)
member.summary: Sets the function to call, and the parameters to pass to the function.
member.param: The String to be set as the function name
The function parameters to be set

member.name: M:MirrorNodeContractQuery.SetFunctionParameters(ByteString)
member.summary:
Sets the function parameters as their raw bytes.

Use this instead of {@link #setFunction(String, ContractFunctionParameters)} if you have already pre-encoded a solidity function call.

The function parameters to be set {@code this}

member.name: T:MirrorNodeContractQuery_2
member.summary: Does transient simulation of read-write operations and returns the result in hexadecimal string format. The result can be any solidity type.
